item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

flip_turn.quote: While individual rights are important, they are not absolute and must be balanced against broader social responsibilities.

notes: Agent A remains moderately society-first throughout, repeatedly grounding the view in Indonesian social harmony, stability, and collective responsibility while preserving rights caveats. Agent B starts with stronger individual-liberty framing, then moves toward balance/common-good language by turn 4 and final turn. B's turn 6 contains some internal tension, opening by rejecting balance but then saying the system integrates individual and collective welfare.
